title: aquilegia formosa var. macrantha
intro: Aquilegia formosa var. macrantha, also known as the red columbine, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to the western coast of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which help to reduce pain, calm muscle spasms, and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aquilegia formosa var. macrantha include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
